name: NSF Regional Innovation Engines
description: Largest NSF place-based innovation program. Funds regional technology ecosystems over 10 years. Requires multi-sector coalitions demonstrating regional coordination.

name: EDA Tech Hubs
description: Designates and funds technology hubs to strengthen regional competitiveness in key technology areas. Requires demonstration of existing technology cluster.

name: DOT Safe Streets and Roads for All (SS4A)
description: Funds comprehensive safety action plans and implementation projects to prevent roadway deaths and serious injuries.

name: DOT Smart Freight & Border Mobility Pilot
description: Pilots smart freight infrastructure at border crossings including automated inspection, connected vehicle technology, and real-time traffic management.

name: USDA Rural Water & Waste Disposal Grants
description: Grants and loans for drinking water, sanitary sewer, solid waste disposal, and stormwater drainage in rural areas and colonias.

name: EPA Border Water Infrastructure Program
description: Funds water and wastewater infrastructure within 100 km of the US-Mexico border. Specifically targets colonias and underserved border communities.

name: HUD Community Development Block Grant (CDBG)
description: Formula-based funding for community development activities benefiting low-to-moderate income residents. Flexible use including housing, infrastructure, and economic development.

name: DOD Defense Manufacturing Communities Program
description: Supports communities with significant defense manufacturing by investing in workforce, infrastructure, and supply chain resilience.

name: SBIR/STTR Program
description: Small business innovation research grants across all federal R&D agencies. Phase I ($100-275K), Phase II ($500K-$2M). STTR requires university partnership.

name: HRSA Health Workforce Programs
description: Funds training programs for health professionals in underserved areas. Specific programs for border health, behavioral health, and community health workers.

name: NSF Broadening Participation in Computing
description: Supports programs that increase participation of underrepresented groups in computing. Aligns with UTEP's Hispanic-serving institution mission.

name: DOE Energy Efficiency and Renewable Energy Grants
description: Funds research in solar, wind, grid modernization, energy storage, and building efficiency. Specific opportunities for arid region and border community applications.

name: NTIA Broadband Equity, Access, and Deployment (BEAD)
description: $42.45B program administered through states for broadband infrastructure deployment. Texas and New Mexico both received allocations. Prioritizes unserved and underserved areas.

name: NASA Space Technology Research Grants
description: Funds early-stage space technology research including materials, propulsion, in-space manufacturing, and autonomous systems.

name: CPRIT (Cancer Prevention and Research Institute of Texas)
description: $6B bond-funded cancer research program. Funds research, prevention, and commercialization. Only available to Texas-based entities.

name: Governor's University Research Initiative (GURI)
description: Competitive matching grants to recruit distinguished researchers to Texas universities. Designed to enhance research competitiveness and reputation.

name: Texas Research Incentive Program (TRIP)
description: Matches private gifts for research at emerging research universities. 50-100% match depending on gift size. Accelerates endowment growth.

name: Comprehensive Research Fund (CRF)
description: Formula funding for emerging research universities based on research expenditures, doctoral graduates, and other metrics. Primary state research infrastructure support.

name: Texas Semiconductor Innovation Fund
description: Aligned with federal CHIPS Act. Funds semiconductor R&D, workforce training, and supply chain development in Texas.

name: NM Economic Development Department Grants
description: Various programs supporting economic development, JTIP workforce training, and community development in New Mexico.

name: NM Environment Department Water Trust Fund
description: Funds water and wastewater infrastructure projects in New Mexico. Prioritizes systems serving disadvantaged communities.

name: NADB (North American Development Bank)
description: Binational institution funding environmental infrastructure within 100 km of the US-Mexico border. Low-interest loans and grants for water, wastewater, air quality, and clean energy.

name: BECC (Border Environment Cooperation Commission)
description: Certifies and funds environmental infrastructure projects in the US-Mexico border region. Works in tandem with NADB financing.

name: DOE National Lab Partnership Programs
description: Various programs enabling university-national lab collaboration including CRADAs, user facility access, and joint research appointments.

name: AFWERX / AFRL Innovation Programs
description: Air Force innovation programs including Spark, Prime, and STRATFI. Rapid prototype-to-production pathways for defense technologies.

name: Manufacturing USA Institute Participation
description: National network of manufacturing innovation institutes. Membership enables access to shared R&D facilities, workforce programs, and pre-competitive research.

name: EDA Public Works and Economic Adjustment Assistance
description: Flexible EDA grants for infrastructure, planning, and economic adjustment in distressed communities. Border regions are priority areas.

name: NSF SBIR/STTR Supplements for EPSCoR States
description: Supplemental funding for SBIR/STTR awardees in EPSCoR states including New Mexico. Enhances competitiveness of NM-based innovation.
